title: DataProjects Object
description: Collection object that provides a list of all available projects.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new project in the parent hub.

methods.name: asArray
methods.description: Get the current list of all projects.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: itemById
methods.description: Returns the project specified using the ID of the project.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of projects in this collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: DataProjects.add Method
add.description: Creates a new project in the parent hub.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = dataProjects_var.add(name)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = dataProjects_var.add(name, purpose, contributors)

add.return_description: Returns the created DataProject object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the project. This is the name visible to the user.

parameters.name: purpose
parameters.description: Optional description of the purpose of the project. An empty string can be used to not specify a purpose.   This is an optional argument whose default value is "".

parameters.name: contributors
parameters.description: Optional list of contributors where the list consists of email addresses separated by a comma. An empty string can be used to not specify any contributors.   This is an optional argument whose default value is "".

asArray.title: DataProjects.asArray Method
asArray.description: Get the current list of all projects.
asArray.return_description: Returns the current list of all projects.

classType.title: DataProjects.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: DataProjects.item Method
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the project to return. The first project in the list has an index of 0.

itemById.title: DataProjects.itemById Method
itemById.description: Returns the project specified using the ID of the project.
itemById.return_description: Returns the project or null if a project with the specified ID is not found.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the project to return. This is the same ID used by the APS Data Management API.

count.title: DataProjects.count Property
count.description: The number of projects in this collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = dataProjects_var.count

isValid.title: DataProjects.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = dataProjects_var.isValid

objectType.title: DataProjects.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = dataProjects_var.objectType
